examples.title: Calculate Air Quality Index (AQI)
examples.description: Basic algorithm to determine AQI category based on PM2.5 concentration
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateAQI
INPUT: pm25_concentration
OUTPUT: AQI_category, health_advisory

BEGIN
 IF pm25_concentration <= 12 THEN
 AQI_category = "Good"
 health_advisory = "Air quality is satisfactory"
 ELSE IF pm25_concentration <= 35.4 THEN
 AQI_category = "Moderate"
 health_advisory = "Acceptable for most"
 ELSE IF pm25_concentration <= 55.4 THEN
 AQI_category = "Unhealthy for Sensitive"
 health_advisory = "Sensitive groups should limit outdoor activity"
 ELSE IF pm25_concentration <= 150.4 THEN
 AQI_category = "Unhealthy"
 health_advisory = "Everyone may experience effects"
 ELSE IF pm25_concentration <= 250.4 THEN
 AQI_category = "Very Unhealthy"
 health_advisory = "Health warnings of emergency conditions"
 ELSE
 AQI_category = "Hazardous"
 health_advisory = "Emergency alert - stay indoors"
 END IF
 RETURN AQI_category, health_advisory
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm categorizes air quality based on PM2.5 concentration using EPA's AQI breakpoints.

examples.title: Pollutant Dispersion Calculation
examples.description: Calculate ground-level concentration using Gaussian dispersion model
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GaussianDispersion
INPUT: Q (emission rate), u (wind speed), H (stack height)
 σy, σz (dispersion coefficients)
OUTPUT: C (ground-level concentration)

BEGIN
 // Simplified Gaussian plume model
 // C = (Q / (2*π*u*σy*σz)) * exp(-H²/(2*σz²))
 
 denominator = 2 * 3.14159 * u * σy * σz
 vertical_term = (H * H) / (2 * σz * σz)
 concentration = Q / denominator * exp(-vertical_term)
 
 RETURN concentration
END

// Note: σy and σz depend on atmospheric stability
ALGORITHM GetDispersionCoefficients
INPUT: distance, stability_class
OUTPUT: σy, σz
BEGIN
 // Look-up table based on Pasquill-Gifford-Turner method
 // σy and σz values increase with distance
 // Stability: A (very unstable) to F (very stable)
 RETURN sigma_y, sigma_z
END
examples.explanation: The Gaussian dispersion model estimates pollutant concentration downwind from a source, accounting for wind speed, emission rate, and atmospheric stability.
